title: Uber in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Yes, Uber works in Berlin, but not the way it does at home.** German law lets Uber operate only as a licensed private-hire car service, not a taxi. There are no street hails and no Uber ranks: you book in the app, and a professional driver in an ordinary car comes to you.

**Uber, Bolt and FreeNow are your three main apps.** Bolt is usually the cheapest, Uber is the most familiar, and FreeNow mostly books real Berlin **taxis** on the regulated meter with app payment and tracking.

**A hidden rule thins out supply.** Private-hire cars must return to base after each ride (the Rückkehrpflicht rule), so at night, in the rain, or far from the centre you will often see surge prices, long waits, or "no cars available".

**Prices are app-quoted, not metered.** Uber and Bolt show a fixed fare before you book; a taxi runs a meter from about EUR 4.30. A short central hop is roughly EUR 10-18, and **BER Airport to the centre is about EUR 50-80** by app depending on demand.

**From BER Airport, the train usually wins.** The Airport Express (FEX) and S-Bahn reach central Berlin in ~30-45 minutes on one ABC ticket for a few euros. Uber, Bolt and FreeNow all pick up at the **Terminal 1** ride-app zone if you prefer a car.

**Pay in-app or by card; tipping is optional.** Rounding up about 5-10 percent is normal and appreciated, but never compulsory. For most central sightseeing, the U-Bahn still beats a car on both price and speed.
